question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 15.00% on 2026-04-25 for the Metaculus question "Will the U.S. conduct a ground invasion of Iran before May 2026?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the Metaculus CP for a US ground invasion of Iran (before May 2026) will exceed 15% on April 25. The CP is currently exactly at 15.00%, making this extremely sensitive to any movement. The US-Iran war has a fragile ceasefire expiring April 22 (3 days before resolution), peace talks have failed, and Trump has imposed a naval blockade. These escalatory dynamics likely push some forecasters to update upward, but time decay (only ~6 days left for the underlying event after April 25) creates strong downward pressure. On balance, the ceasefire expiration is the key catalyst that slightly favors YES.

factors.description: CP is exactly at 15.00% threshold — any upward movement crosses it, making YES easier to achieve than if CP were well below
Ceasefire expires April 22, 3 days before resolution — if it collapses without extension, forecasters will likely update upward on invasion risk
Peace talks failed April 12 and Trump announced naval blockade April 13 — escalatory signals that could push CP up
Pentagon had earlier planned limited ground operations (Kharg Island raids) — background knowledge raises floor for CP
CP dropped from 60% to 15% in one week — high volatility suggests it could easily bounce back above 15% with any escalation
Time decay: only ~6 days remain after April 25 for invasion before May 1, and 5 consecutive days required — makes actual invasion near-impossible, pulling CP down
No US ground troops have entered Iran; war has been entirely air/naval — status quo favors CP staying low
Trump admin 'keen to avoid prolonged military engagement'; anti-forever-wars branding
Both sides still communicating, second round of talks being planned — diplomatic path reduces escalation risk
Manifold markets show 33% for ground invasion before end of 2026 — meaningful residual risk perception in broader timeframes

reasoning:
The reflection passed with approval. Let me finalize my forecast.

## Summary of Analysis

**Question**: Will the Metaculus CP for "US ground invasion of Iran before May 2026" be above 15% on April 25?

**Key situation**: The US and Iran are in an active war (since Feb 28, 2026). A two-week ceasefire began April 8 but peace talks failed April 12. Trump announced a naval blockade April 13. The ceasefire expires April 22 — just 3 days before this question resolves.

**Core tension**: The CP dropped from 60% → 15% due to the ceasefire, but the ceasefire is fragile and about to expire with no deal. The threshold is exactly at the current CP level, so any upward movement resolves YES. However, time decay is powerful — by April 25, only 6 days remain for the underlying event, making a ground invasion near-impossible logistically.

**Scenario decomposition**:
- If ceasefire collapses (~55%): CP likely bounces above 15% (~78% conditional probability)
- If ceasefire extends (~45%): CP likely stays near/below 15% (~27% conditional probability of exceeding)
- Combined: ~55% × 78% + ~45% × 27% ≈ 55%

Adjusting slightly upward because the blockade itself is an escalatory development that may push forecasters to update even before April 22.

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42542) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-04-25 18:48:36 is higher than 15.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 15.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-04-25 18:48:36, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
